episode.title: The Not It Protocol – Nature's Least Fair Algorithm
episode.tone: calm David Attenborough-style narration with dry academic humor

voice.desc: British male narrator, calm, deep, documentary

beats.narration: In the vast and chaotic ecosystem of human cooperation, there exists a most remarkable survival mechanism. It is swift. It is ruthless. It is the 'Not It' protocol.
beats.direction: Soft orchestral intro. Gentle camera pan across a human habitat (living room, office).
beats.footage_prompt: wide cinematic shot of a cozy living room and open-plan office, warm daylight, nature documentary framing

beats.narration: Triggered by the mere mention of an undesirable task, the 'Not It' sequence begins. Within milliseconds, equilibrium collapses into a frenzy of vocal exclusion.
beats.direction: Rising tension. Close-ups of faces reacting to the trash task.
beats.footage_prompt: close-up of human faces reacting with sudden alarm, documentary lighting, shallow depth of field

beats.narration: Each participant scrambles to utter the sacred words: 'Not it.' It is a contest of reflexes, not reason.
beats.direction: Slow-motion montage of people shouting Not it.
beats.footage_prompt: slow motion montage of people shouting and raising hands, energetic, documentary

beats.narration: In this game, fairness is a myth. The fastest and loudest survive. The slowest become 'It.'
beats.direction: Dramatic pause; zoom on shocked loser.
beats.footage_prompt: dramatic slow zoom on a single shocked person's face, desaturated, cinematic

beats.narration: Observe the defeated specimen, isolated, burdened, and universally pitied. Moments ago, they were one of the pack. Now, they alone must carry the terrible weight of taking out the bins.
beats.direction: Sad music, the 'It' person doing chores.
beats.footage_prompt: lone person reluctantly carrying a trash bag at dusk, melancholy, cool vignette, documentary

beats.narration: Social scientists have long marveled at the elegance of this mechanism. No formal governance. No voting. No justice whatsoever. And yet, it works. Efficiently. Instantly. Mercilessly.
beats.footage_prompt: clean sterile infographic of mock scientific graphs and charts, white background, fairness versus efficiency

beats.narration: Conflicts do arise, of course. When declarations overlap, primitive arbitration ensues: yelling, re-dos, or blaming the slowest-looking person.
beats.footage_prompt: group of people arguing animatedly and pointing fingers, warm tint, documentary chaos

beats.narration: In rare cases, a preemptive 'Not it' may be issued before a task even exists. These are swiftly nullified by the community, unless everyone's too tired to care.
beats.footage_prompt: person lazily lounging on a couch raising one finger, low energy, low contrast, documentary

beats.narration: Across generations and continents, this protocol endures, a testament to humanity's eternal preference for fast unfairness over slow fairness.
beats.footage_prompt: bright colorful global montage of diverse people in many countries, uplifting, documentary

beats.narration: Truly, a wonder of social evolution. And while some dream of reforming it, perhaps introducing equity or randomized assignment, most agree such efforts would simply ruin it.
beats.footage_prompt: slow fade to black over an empty quiet room, sepia, reverent, documentary

titlecard.text: The Not It Protocol – Nature's Least Fair Algorithm
titlecard.subtitle: A Comedy Mockumentary
